sections.paragraph:
χαρακόω (χάραξ) 1 aor. ἐχαράκωσα; pf. pass. ptc. κεχαρακωμένος fence in (with stakes) (Aeschin. 3, 140; Plut., Cleom. 813 [20, 1]; POxy 729, 23 [II a.d.]; Jer 39:2) τὶ someth. a vineyard Hs 5, 2, 2f; 5, 4, 1. Pass. 5, 2, 5 (cp. Is 5:2, where the verb prob. means ‘provide w. stakes [for individual vines]’, a sense which is excluded for H by Hs 5, 5, 3 [s. συγκρατέω 2]).—DELG s.v. χαράσσω I.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Cleom Cleom , II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

someth. someth. = something

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

prob. prob. = probable, probably

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1077
